$comment: Hand-maintained companion to config-schema.json. It carries what reflection over the Config struct cannot see: which fields belong on the wizard's basic vs advanced step, one-line help, the expected value FORMAT for fields validated imperatively (IP/CIDR/host:port/community — not by regex, so absent from the generated schema's `pattern`), and behavioural flags. Keys are dotted yaml paths; array elements use the field name with no index (e.g. hostgroups.name, bgp.neighbors.address). Flags: `secret` — the field names an environment VARIABLE, the wizard must collect a name and never a value; `defaultWhenAbsent` — value the engine assumes when the key is omitted (the wizard must respect it, e.g. NEVER emit dry_run:false without an explicit, warned opt-in — prefer omitting the key); `reloadImmutable` — changing it needs a process restart, a SIGHUP reload is rejected; `serverVerified` — points at a server-side file the browser cannot check, so `kapkan -check-config` on the host is the real check. TestOverlayKeysExist guarantees every key here still resolves in config-schema.json; reconcile against engine/internal/config/config.go via /sync-wizard.

dry_run.description: When true (the default, including when the key is absent), mitigation is simulated and never announced. Keep it on until detection is validated against live telemetry.

listen.sflow.description: UDP listen address for sFlow, e.g. ":6343". At least one of sflow/netflow is required.

listen.netflow.description: UDP listen address for NetFlow v5/v9 and IPFIX (shared socket), e.g. ":2055".

sampling.default_rate.description: Sampling rate assumed when an exporter does not report its own. Wrong here scales EVERY threshold — set it to what your routers actually export.

sampling.boundary_debug.description: Temporarily export the kapkan_engine_boundary_debug_bytes_total{exporter,iface,dir} metric (bytes toward/from protected hosts per exporter+interface) to discover which interfaces are the external boundary. Not cardinality-bounded — enable briefly, then turn off.

sampling.boundary.exporter.crossField: Each exporter may appear once; external_ifindexes must be non-empty. Exporters without a boundary entry keep counting every sample.
sampling.boundary.exporter.description: Sampler/agent IP this boundary rule applies to.

sampling.boundary.external_ifindexes.description: ifIndex values of this exporter's external (uplink/border) interfaces. A flow is counted toward a protected host only when it crosses one — inbound on an external input interface, outbound on an external output one — deduplicating flows seen at multiple vantage points.

sampling.boundary.egress_sampling.description: Halves the boundary-counted rate to undo an in+out double-count. Default false. Set true ONLY if calibration shows it: compare the external ifIndex's boundary_debug rate to the interface's real counter and enable only when it reads ~2x. Do NOT set it merely because the device runs `sflow sample output` — on some topologies (e.g. MLAG) the egress copy uses a different input interface and is not double-counted, so true would under-count by half.

networks.crossField: Prefixes must not overlap each other. Detection applies ONLY to destinations inside these.

protected_whitelist.description: Addresses that are never banned, regardless of traffic (gateways, nameservers).

flow_sources.description: Optional allowlist of trusted flow-exporter source addresses; bounds metric cardinality, does not affect detection.

thresholds.pps.description: Per-host packets/sec threshold (real, unsampled units). Required, > 0.

thresholds.flows_per_sec.description: Per-host flows/sec threshold. Required, > 0. NetFlow/IPFIX only: sFlow exports one sample per packet (no flow aggregation), so sFlow-sourced hosts always report 0 and this never fires for them — use pps/tcp_syn_pps/udp_pps.

thresholds.tcp_syn_pps.description: Optional pure-SYN packets/sec limit; 0 or absent disables it.

thresholds.udp_pps.description: Optional UDP packets/sec limit; 0 or absent disables it.

thresholds_outgoing.pps.crossField: If the block is present, set at least one threshold.
thresholds_outgoing.pps.description: Detects attacks ORIGINATED by protected hosts (compromised machines). Absent, outgoing traffic is not counted.

ban.ttl_seconds.description: Every announcement auto-withdraws after this many seconds. There are no permanent bans.

ban.unban_hysteresis_seconds.description: Traffic must stay below threshold this long before a ban is withdrawn (anti-flap).

ban.max_active_bans.description: Hard cap on simultaneous bans; new bans past the cap are refused.

ban.fallback.description: What to do when a flowspec/divert announce is REJECTED by the peer: 'blackhole' (the default) degrades to an RTBH route so the victim is still mitigated; 'none' rejects the ban, leaving the victim undefended.

ban.max_banned_fraction.description: Refuse new bans once more than this share (0–1, per address family) of the protected space is blackholed — bounds the blast radius of a poisoned baseline or spoofed-source storm. 0 (default) disables the guard.

ban.max_bans_per_window.description: Storm guard: at most this many NEW bans may be created per ban_window_seconds. 0 (default) disables it.

ban.ban_window_seconds.crossField: Required (> 0) when max_bans_per_window is set; ignored otherwise.
ban.ban_window_seconds.description: The sliding window for max_bans_per_window, in seconds.

ban.state_file.description: Writable path where active bans are persisted, so they are rehydrated and re-announced on startup — paired with bgp.graceful_restart this keeps mitigation up across a restart. Empty (default) disables persistence; the systemd unit provides a writable dir via StateDirectory=kapkan.

mitigation.description: Default mitigation method. blackhole drops all victim traffic; flowspec is surgical; divert sends traffic to a scrubbing center.

flowspec.action.description: FlowSpec rule action. rate_limit requires flowspec.rate_mbps > 0.

flowspec.rate_mbps.crossField: Required and > 0 only when action is rate_limit.

flowspec.source_anchored.description: When on, a flowspec rule pins BOTH the victim (destination) AND a dominant attacker source from the attack sample, dropping only the attackers and sparing the victim's legitimate clients — but only when the sample is concentrated (see min_source_concentration); otherwise it falls back to a victim-anchored rule. Default off.

flowspec.min_source_concentration.description: Share (0–1) of sampled attack packets the dominant sources must cover, within the 8-rule budget, before source anchoring is used. Omitted/0 means 0.8 when source_anchored is on. A diffuse attack (reflection/spoofing across many sources) stays below it and falls back to victim-anchored.

carpet.aggregation_prefix_v4.description: Supernet length per-host incoming rates fold into for carpet-bombing (subnet-spread) detection (default /24).

carpet.aggregation_prefix_v6.description: IPv6 aggregation supernet length for carpet-bombing detection (default /48).

carpet.min_hosts.description: Fan-out gate: at least this many distinct hosts in the prefix must carry traffic before a carpet attack fires, so one heavy host (already caught per-host) is not reported as a carpet bomb (minimum 2).

carpet.thresholds.pps.description: Aggregate packets/sec threshold for the whole prefix (summed over its hosts). 0 disables this metric; set at least one carpet threshold. Should be well above the per-host pps.

carpet.thresholds.mbps.description: Aggregate megabits/sec threshold for the prefix. 0 disables it.

carpet.thresholds.flows_per_sec.description: Aggregate flows/sec threshold for the prefix. 0 disables it. NetFlow/IPFIX only (sFlow-sourced hosts report 0).

carpet.mitigation.description: Auto-mitigate the aggregation prefix: empty (default) is alert-only; 'flowspec' announces a FlowSpec rule matching the attack vector on the whole prefix (surgical); 'blackhole' announces an RTBH route for the prefix (drops all of it). EITHER method refuses a prefix containing a protected_whitelist address — the whitelist guarantee is absolute.

carpet.max_active_prefix_bans.description: Cap on simultaneous carpet (prefix) bans, separate from ban.max_active_bans (host bans) so neither starves the other. A prefix ban also counts its full address span toward ban.max_banned_fraction.

escalation.after_seconds.crossField: The first rung must be 0; rungs strictly increase; a ladder may only hold or strengthen (none < flowspec < divert < blackhole).
escalation.after_seconds.description: Delay from attack start at which this rung applies.

escalation.action.description: Rung action. Supersedes the single `mitigation` method when escalation is set.

scrubbing.next_hop.description: Scrubbing center IPv4 next-hop for the divert action.

scrubbing.next_hop6.crossField: Required to divert when IPv6 space is protected.

scrubbing.community.description: Optional divert community (ASN:value); the next-hop does the rerouting.

baseline.factor.description: Multiplies the learned baseline into the effective threshold (1.5..100, default 3).

baseline.warmup_seconds.description: Observation time before a host's baseline gates detection (0..86400, default 600).

baseline.floor.pps.description: Minimum effective pps threshold — a quiet host's tiny baseline must not make detection hair-trigger. Required, > 0.

hostgroups.name.description: Group name (log/JSON/header-safe charset). Must be unique and not 'global'.

hostgroups.networks.crossField: Each prefix must sit inside a top-level networks entry and not collide with another group.
hostgroups.networks.description: Prefixes this group owns (longest-prefix match wins).

hostgroups.calculation.crossField: A total group alerts only and never auto-bans; ban:true and explicit flowspec/divert are rejected for it.
hostgroups.calculation.description: per_host evaluates each host; total sums the group's traffic.

hostgroups.thresholds.pps.description: Per-group pps override; omit the block to inherit the global thresholds.

hostgroups.mitigation.description: Per-group mitigation method; empty inherits the global default.

hostgroups.ban.description: Automatic RTBH for the group's hosts (default true). Must not be true for a total group.

hostgroups.tenant.description: Optional owner label; a tenant-scoped API token sees and mutates only its own groups.

tenant.description: Optional tenant label for the implicit global/fallback group.

samples.enabled.description: Continuous flow buffer that attaches dominant sources/ports to each attack.

samples.buffer_flows.description: Total capacity of the recent-flows ring (256..1048576, default 65536). Sizing changes require a restart.

samples.flows_per_attack.description: Raw flow records attached to one attack event (1..500, default 20).

bgp.router_id.description: BGP router ID; must be an IPv4 dotted-quad.

bgp.next_hop6.description: IPv6 blackhole next-hop (required if IPv6 space is protected and blackholed).

bgp.community.description: RTBH community your upstream honors, as ASN:value.

bgp.graceful_restart.enabled.description: Advertise BGP Graceful Restart so a peer RETAINS kapkan's mitigation routes across a restart (e.g. an upgrade) instead of flushing them. On by default; advertising costs nothing against a peer that doesn't support it. Takes effect on (re)start.

bgp.graceful_restart.restart_seconds.description: How long the peer holds kapkan's routes as stale while the session is down. 0 uses the default (120); capped at 4095.

bgp.graceful_restart.long_lived.description: Enable Long-Lived Graceful Restart (RFC 9494), extending retention beyond restart_seconds. Off by default — long LLGR with FlowSpec can cause transient forwarding loops.

bgp.graceful_restart.long_lived_stale_se.crossField: Used only when long_lived is set.
bgp.graceful_restart.long_lived_stale_se.description: LLGR stale time in seconds. 0 uses the default (3600, 1h); capped at 86400 (24h).

geoip.enabled.description: Enable GeoIP/ASN attribution of attack-sample sources.

geoip.asn_database.description: Path to a GeoLite2-ASN.mmdb file on the engine host.

geoip.country_database.description: Path to a GeoLite2-Country.mmdb file on the engine host.

storage.clickhouse.url.description: ClickHouse HTTP endpoint, e.g. http://127.0.0.1:8123. Empty disables persistence.

storage.clickhouse.database.description: ClickHouse database name (identifier charset, default kapkan).

storage.clickhouse.username_env.description: Env var holding the ClickHouse username.

storage.clickhouse.password_env.description: Env var holding the ClickHouse password.

notify.telegram.token_env.description: Env var holding the Telegram bot token (never the token itself).

notify.telegram.chat_id.description: Telegram chat ID to post alerts to (look it up via @getmyid_bot).

notify.slack.webhook_url.description: Slack incoming webhook URL (https; the path is a bearer secret).

notify.email.smtp_host.crossField: When set, from and at least one to recipient are required.
notify.email.smtp_host.description: SMTP server host:port; empty disables email.

notify.email.username_env.description: Env var holding the SMTP username.

notify.email.password_env.description: Env var holding the SMTP password.

notify.exec.command.description: Absolute path to an executable run on each attack event; verified at load on the engine host.

notify.exec.format.description: Invocation convention: kapkan (event argv + JSON stdin) or fastnetmon (FastNetMon notify_script contract).

api.listen.description: REST API and metrics listen address (default 127.0.0.1:8080).

api.dashboard.description: Serve the embedded web UI on the API listener.

api.token_env.crossField: Mutually exclusive with api.tokens. Required before exposing the listener beyond localhost.
api.token_env.description: Env var holding a single operator bearer token.

api.tokens.token_env.description: Env var holding this token's secret.

api.tokens.tenant.description: Optional tenant scope; must match a tenant used by some hostgroup.

update_check.enabled.description: Opt-in check for a newer kapkan release via the GitHub Releases API. Off by default (no egress); the running version is always exposed locally regardless. Takes effect on (re)start.

update_check.interval_seconds.description: Poll interval for the update check. 0 uses the default (21600, 6h); floored at 3600 (1h).

update_check.channel.description: Which releases count: 'stable' (latest non-prerelease) or 'prerelease' (includes -rc tags).

update_check.url.description: Override the releases endpoint (mirror/proxy). Empty derives the public GitHub API URL from channel.

update_check.notify.description: Also send 'update available' through the configured notify channels (Telegram/webhook/Slack/email) the first time a new version is seen.
